title: Fantasia 2014
body:
Two-day immersive art/music event at Rhinoceropolis, opening June 6, 2014. Co-curated by **Colin Ward** and **Stephan Herrera**. 

Westword described it as "part art exhibition, part concert, all interactive crazy aquarium experience." Colin described the visual theme as "a laser-tag arena/coral reef/planetarium/aquarium sort of feel." In the month leading up to the event, Rhinoceropolis was transformed — rooms and outdoor spaces "morphed into multi-level, color- and light-saturated sculpture gardens and installations from local and national artists."

A massive white tarp angled down from the roof into the backyard to create a projection canvas for video-manipulation duo **VidKidz**. The front room housed a continuous loop of video art clips curated by Herrera. Collaborators included **Luke Leavitt**, **Ben Donehower**, **Laura Conway**, and many more. Designed as an intimate, welcoming event for people who had never visited a DIY space.

Colin's Cargo Collective portfolio preserved an entire F2014-* poster series: F2014-THA-ART-, F2014-SPIDER-DREAM, AIR SHAMAN, AIRPORT, CASH CONTROL, CLUB GLOBE, RAPTOR JAZZ, HOW2GET-WHAT-U-WANT4.

A separate Westword piece, "Cave Rave Will Set a Sweaty Stage on the Path to Fantasia," served as a preview/lead-up to the event.
